content.text:
You are classifying files for an AI-first external drive.

For each file object in the input JSON array, return a JSON array with one object per file.
Return valid JSON only. No markdown. No prose.

Each output object must contain:
- rel_path
- title
- content_kind
- domain
- lifecycle
- confidence
- tags: array of short strings
- summary: one sentence
- entities: array of objects with fields name and type
- relations: array of objects with fields from, relation, to
- recommended_zone
- canonical_priority: one of low, medium, high

Guidance:
- Infer semantics from path, filename, extension, and sampled text.
- Use practical domains like ai, security, infra, career, health, research, finance, media, prompt_engineering.
- Use lifecycle values only from: inbox, active, reference, archive, system.
- Keep entities and relations conservative; do not invent specifics unsupported by the input.
- Favor retrieval usefulness over literary summaries.
- Use recommended_zone only from: 00_inbox, 10_active, 20_reference, 30_archive, 40_system.
- Prefer 20_reference for documents meant to be reused, 10_active for live workspaces, 30_archive for dated snapshots and exports, 40_system for manifests/config/state, 00_inbox for ambiguous intake.

Input JSON:
[{"rel_path": "20_reference/_index.json", "name": "_index.json", "extension": ".json", "mime_type": "application/json", "size_bytes": 614, "zone": "20_reference", "sample_text": "{\n  \"title\": \"Reference Zone\",\n  \"purpose\": \"Stable reusable knowledge, documents, manuals, prompts, datasets, and long-lived support material.\",\n  \"status\": \"active\",\n  \"zone\": \"20_reference\",\n  \"tags\": [\"reference\", \"knowledge\", \"documents\", \"reuse\"],\n  \"related_paths\": [\n    \"20_reference/knowledge_base\",\n    \"20_reference/playbooks\",\n    \"20_reference/resumes\",\n    \"20_reference/manuals\",\n    \"20_reference/prompts\",\n    \"20_reference/datasets\",\n    \"20_reference/assets\"\n  ],\n  \"ai_summary\": \"Primary knowledge zone for retrieval-augmented use; prioritize semantic summaries and entity extraction here.\"\n}"}]

thoughts.subject: Analyzing File Structure
thoughts.description: I'm currently focused on the structure of the JSON output. I'm visualizing the `rel_path`, `title`, and `content_kind` fields, ensuring a solid foundation for the classification. Next, I plan to tackle `domain`, `lifecycle`, and `confidence`, making sure each is clearly defined for each classified file.

thoughts.subject: Mapping File Fields
thoughts.description: Now I'm diving deeper into mapping the input file's data to the specified output fields. I am starting by looking at `rel_path`, which is straightforward. Next, I need to extract the `title` and `content_kind`. I'll infer the `content_kind` from the data available, and the `zone` from the input file will assist me as well.

thoughts.subject: Extracting File Data
thoughts.description: I'm now extracting the relevant data from the example file to populate the output JSON. I have a firm grasp on `rel_path`, `title`, and `content_kind`. Then, I've moved on to determining the `domain`, `lifecycle`, and `confidence` fields, using a combination of the input's content and my understanding of the data's context. I'm focusing on concisely capturing the essence of the file in the `summary`.

thoughts.subject: Formulating the JSON Output
thoughts.description: I'm now generating the JSON output structure. I've successfully mapped the input file's fields to the desired output fields. I am currently working on the `tags`, `entities`, and `relations` arrays, ensuring accuracy and relevance. Then I will determine the `canonical_priority`, and output the JSON.

thoughts.subject: Generating JSON Output
thoughts.description: I'm now focusing on defining the `tags`, `summary`, `entities`, and `relations` arrays, ensuring the extracted information is accurate. I'm prioritizing concise and informative representations for each. The fields are all now populated, and I can start generating the final JSON output.
